goldenDemos.outcome: Agent user searches catalog, compares products, proposes add-to-cart, requests approval, mutates app-owned cart state, and emits observable workflow state.
acceptance: Uses browser-local cascade when available.
Searches catalog before product claims.
Does not mutate cart before approval.
Executes approved add-to-cart through host-owned app state.
Shows coherent fallback states when no model is available.

goldenDemos.outcome: Agent user searches accounts, evaluates role/policy context, proposes risky admin changes, gates them through approval, and records audit/telemetry.
acceptance: Respects role and permission state.
Searches account records before recommending changes.
Requires approval for plan changes and suspensions.
Records audit-worthy action details.
Keeps host app as source of truth.

goldenDemos.outcome: Agent user retrieves product knowledge, cites sources, refuses unsupported claims, only proposes transactional actions when grounded in policy/docs, and uses cascade/fallback states honestly.
acceptance: Uses retrieval before factual answers.
Cites sources for supported claims.
Refuses unsupported claims.
Only proposes transactional actions when the retrieved docs support the action.
Identifies the runtime/model/assistant relationship accurately.
Does not reduce Edgekit to docs search in adoption answers.

goldenDemos.outcome: Agent user operates a small public site: answers grounded questions, explains its capability mode, uses browser-local cascade when available, refuses unsupported identity/disambiguation claims, and only proposes transactional actions when grounded in site policy.
acceptance: Known local-model browser does not stay in Basic mode.
User can understand and change capability/cascade mode.
Unsupported claims such as Ohio, robots, and rockets are refused.
Identity questions are answered from configured assistant/runtime identity.
No-model fallback is labeled as fallback, not the product experience.
Only proposes transactional actions when the retrieved site evidence supports the action.

goldenDemos.outcome: Agent user starts local (data gathering, validation, proposal), detects need for server capability or higher assurance, makes explicit user-facing mode disclosure + handoff request, and Worker completes the transactional outcome (mutation, external system call, final state) with full telemetry and audit across the handoff boundary.
acceptance: Local/browser mode handles bounded app tasks (data gathering, validation, proposal).
App-owned Worker handles tasks that need server capability and completes transactional mutations.
User sees why escalation happened (explicit mode disclosure).
Handoff context is bounded and excludes secrets.
Telemetry records mode transition, proposal, and outcome across boundary.
EDGEKIT-LOOP metadata and boundary guard enforced.
No core boundary leakage; all transactional logic is demo-specific.

labs.purpose: Prototype first-visit and runtime mode-change UX for local model, WebLLM download, cloud fallback, and Basic fallback.
acceptance: Detects browser/local model availability before exposing agent-operable features.
Prompts for an explicit WebLLM download and records download/provider status.
Offers an app-owned cloud/server route when configured.
Labels Basic fallback as setup guidance only, not the product experience.
Records telemetry and audit evidence for first-visit, retry, and mode-change transitions.

labs.purpose: Prove an existing non-agent app can be retrofitted with the smallest host-owned helper/tool surface needed for a governed agent user to complete workflows.
acceptance: Agent reads host app state before workflow claims.
Risky same-day scheduling is blocked without approval.
Approved mutation executes only through host.actions.scheduleSameDayVisit.
Telemetry/audit captures tool calls, approval, mutation, and outcome.
Workarounds are classified as app-specific integration or reusable Edgekit friction.

labs.purpose: Focused lab demonstrating the partial assistance / co-pilot pattern: agent excels at data gathering, analysis, and proposal; user retains final execution/approval. Clear handoff point with boundary guard. Local model path success.
acceptance: Local model path success with agent performing analysis + proposal.
Clear visual and state separation between agent proposal and user execution.
Browser QA captures explicit handoff point evidence.
EDGEKIT-LOOP metadata and boundary guard enforced.
Agent proposal never auto-executes; execution is user-triggered host action only.
